idea.title: Echo Locator
idea.hook: Ping a black void with sonar clicks to map invisible enemies and walls — every ping costs battery and reveals YOU to the things listening.
idea.prototype: Top-down 32x32 grid, screen pitch black except player dot. Click anywhere to emit a circular ping (radius 8) that briefly lights walls + creature silhouettes for 0.6s. Each ping drains 1 of 12 battery. Creatures hear pings and move 1 tile toward you per ping. Goal: reach the exit tile (also invisible — must be discovered). Battery refills at 3 hidden charge nodes. Win = reach exit. Lose = creature touches you OR battery hits 0 with no exit found.
idea.why_novel: No portal game makes the act of LOOKING the central risk — every reveal of the world is also a reveal of yourself, turning information itself into the resource that hunts you back.
idea.non_timer_pressure: battery resource (12 pings) + enemies that close in per ping
idea.lose_condition: Creature reaches player OR battery depletes before exit

synthesizer_brief: Mutation of hidden-info puzzle + horror-tension; reveal IS the risk

alternatives.title: Crack Captain
alternatives.hook: Ram an icebreaker through a hex floe
